name: calendar
description: Date grid built on react-day-picker v9. Supports single, multiple, and range selection modes. Keyboard navigable and localizable.

props.name: selected
props.description: Controlled selected value (Date, Date[], or DateRange depending on mode)

props.name: disabled
props.description: DayPicker Matcher — accepts a Date, Date[], { from, to }, { before | after }, or a (date: Date) => boolean predicate

props.name: showOutsideDays
props.description: Render days from the previous/next month in the grid

props.name: numberOfMonths
props.description: How many months to display side-by-side

props.name: defaultMonth
props.description: The month to render first (uncontrolled). Date object.

props.name: fromDate
props.description: Earliest selectable date (Date). Days before are disabled.

props.name: toDate
props.description: Latest selectable date (Date). Days after are disabled.

props.name: locale
props.description: date-fns Locale object (e.g. `import { es } from 'date-fns/locale'`) for weekday/month labels

props.name: weekStartsOn
props.description: First day of the week (0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday, …, 6 = Saturday)

examples.title: Single date selection
examples.description: Bind a Date state to selected/onSelect

examples.title: Range selection
examples.description: Pick a start and end date

ai.whenToUse: Use for inline date selection UIs, or as the month-grid inside a DatePicker (wrapped in a Popover). Supports single date, multi-date, and range modes.
ai.whenNotToUse: Don't use when only a text date input is needed (use Input type=date). Don't embed inside a very narrow container — the grid needs ~280px min width. For scheduling UIs with time, combine with a separate time picker.
commonMistakes: Passing a string to selected (must be a Date, Date[], or DateRange object)
Forgetting mode prop (default is single, but being explicit avoids confusion)
Overriding classNames completely instead of spreading — loses default styling
Using inside a Server Component without marking the consumer 'use client'
ai.accessibilityNotes: react-day-picker wires aria-label, aria-selected, and keyboard navigation (arrows, Home/End, PageUp/Down). Focus rings on day buttons use the ring token.
